rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS SIU CHAN AND JUSTIN TIN'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 19, 2021. On September 16, 2021 this fully briefed motion was continued for parties to provide courtesy copies of their previously filed papers in support of and in opposition to the demurrer. On October 6, 2021, 3 weeks after the original hearing date, Plaintiff filed a Memorandum and Declaration in Reply in Opposition to Reply. By no later than October 29, 2021 Plaintiff to file a declaration addressing this filing, including the authority for such filing, and addressing why this filing should be considered by the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
